nativeRange.habitat: Seasonally flooded wetlands, marsh edges, slow shallow rivers, and pond margins across tropical lowland South America

decorPreferences: open foreground or midground for runners

summary: Grass-like Amazonian rosette plant generally regarded as an easy foreground or midground carpeter. Spreads readily by runners and is commonly used to form a lawn of narrow green blades.
careNotes: NOMENCLATURE NOTE: This entry covers the trade-named 'Chain Sword Narrow Leaf' or 'Echinodorus quadricostas' / 'E. quadricostatus', which modern taxonomy treats as synonyms of Helanthium bolivianum (Rusby) Lehtonen & Myllys. The species is closely related to and frequently confused with Helanthium tenellum (the 'Pygmy Chain Sword'), and many trade pages - including the assigned ModernAquarium primary - describe the plant biologically as E. tenellus while selling it under the 'quadricostas' name. The two species have very similar appearance and husbandry; the present entry follows the user-supplied taxonomic intent (H. bolivianum). A separate helanthium-tenellum entry already exists for the smaller sister species. Mature blades are commonly reported in the 5-10 cm range under bright light and as foreground carpets, but can stretch to 15-20 cm in lower light or as a midground accent. Generally considered beginner-friendly: tolerates a wide temperature band (reported 20-28 C as the comfortable range, with wider tolerances in nature), pH around 5.5-7.5, and soft to moderately hard water. CO2 is described as optional but generally helpful for dense carpets; reported target concentrations sit around 10-40 mg/l in source literature. A nutrient-rich substrate and root tabs tend to produce the best results, and a balanced all-in-one liquid fertilizer is commonly recommended. Cross-source data sources: ModernAquarium product page (primary; note nomenclature conflation as above) and Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database (secondary, covers the H. bolivianum/quadricostatus species directly). Wikipedia confirmed taxonomy and synonymy. May overlap with future 'Helanthium Bolivianum' entries - same biological species under different trade naming.
breedingNotes: Propagates readily by sending out runners (stolons) that produce daughter rosettes along the substrate, forming the chain that gives the plant its common name. Has been reported to flower when grown emersed.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Trade-named product page sold as 'Echinodorus quadricostas'. Care prose on the page itself references E. tenellus; this conflation is common in the trade. Used for placement, lighting, growth-rate, substrate, and propagation guidance.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters (temp 20-26 C optimal / 10-30 C tolerated, pH 5-7.5, kH 0-14 dKH), CO2 (10-40 mg/l), height (under 5 cm short form, over 20 cm tall form), growth rate (fast), propagation (runners), and foreground-to-midground placement. Authoritative species-level coverage of H. bolivianum/quadricostatus.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Alismataceae) and synonymy: Echinodorus quadricostatus Fassett and Echinodorus bolivianus (Rusby) Holm-Niels are both junior synonyms of Helanthium bolivianum. Native range stated as southern Mexico, Central America, the West Indies, and South America.
